music-players.label: Music Players
music-players.desc: Explore discontinued music players and services—from portable CD players and MiniDisc to early streaming. Timelines, outcomes, and the brands that shaped how we listened.
music-players.descLong: From early MP3 devices to smartphone-era transitions...

faq.q: What counts as a music player?
faq.a: Dedicated portable audio devices and closely related services.

portable-audio.label: Portable Audio
portable-audio.desc: Portable audio devices that came and went—spanning cassette, CD, MiniDisc, and early digital eras. Browse the brands that defined how we listened on the go.
portable-audio.descLong: Spanning cassette, CD, MiniDisc, and digital eras...

scandal.label: Scandal-Ended Brands
scandal.desc: Companies and brands that collapsed due to fraud, misconduct, or major ethical scandals—from Enron and Theranos to FTX. Timelines, fates, and what went wrong.
scandal.descLong: From Enron and WorldCom to Theranos and FTX, these cases show how governance failures and misconduct can erase even famous names. This hub collects timelines, fates, and sources.

revived-brand.label: Revived Brands
revived-brand.desc: Brands that disappeared and came back—sometimes stronger, sometimes changed beyond recognition. Browse the discontinued labels that got a second chance and what happened next.
revived-brand.descLong: Not every defunct brand stays gone. Some return as nostalgia plays, some through new ownership, and some as shadows of their former selves. This topic collects the revivals.

food-service.label: Food Service
food-service.desc: Discontinued restaurant chains, cafeteria concepts, and food-service brands that fed generations before closing. Browse timelines, fates, and what led each one to disappear.
food-service.descLong: From regional diners to national chains, food-service brands often become deeply tied to community memory before they vanish.

energy-drink-era.label: Energy Drink Era
energy-drink-era.desc: The early energy drink market was wild—caffeinated alcohol, extreme formulas, and bold branding that regulators eventually reined in. Browse the brands that defined and ended the era.
energy-drink-era.descLong: Before strict regulations, brands like Sparks and Tilt blended caffeine with alcohol. The FDA crackdown in 2008-2010 reshaped the entire category.

movie-theater.label: Movie Theater
movie-theater.desc: Snack brands, concession staples, and products closely tied to the movie theater experience—some discontinued, some just faded. Browse what we used to eat at the movies.
movie-theater.descLong: Movie theater candy and snacks often outlast the films themselves—until they don't. This topic covers concession-aisle staples and brands tied to the cinema experience.

1920s-brand.label: 1920s Brands
1920s-brand.desc: Brands founded or defined in the 1920s—from Prohibition-era products and early aviation pioneers to the retailers and car marques that shaped American consumer culture.
1920s-brand.descLong: The 1920s were a decade of dramatic economic growth, new consumer technologies, and cultural transformation. Many brands born in this era outlasted the Depression before eventually disappearing.

regulatory-crackdown.label: Regulatory Crackdown
regulatory-crackdown.desc: Brands that were discontinued or drastically changed due to government bans, safety recalls, or regulatory enforcement—including banned food additives, alcohol-caffeine mixes, and unsafe toys.
regulatory-crackdown.descLong: Some products don't fade—they're stopped. Regulatory crackdowns on safety, ingredients, or labeling have ended multiple well-known brands and product categories.

bankruptcy.label: Bankruptcy
bankruptcy.desc: Brands that ended or were reshaped by bankruptcy—including retail chains, airlines, tech companies, and consumer brands that filed Chapter 11 or liquidated. Browse what happened to each.
bankruptcy.descLong: Bankruptcy doesn't always mean the end—some brands restructure and return, while others liquidate entirely. This topic covers the full spectrum of what happens when companies run out of options.

vanished-product.label: Vanished Products
vanished-product.desc: Specific products—not full brands—that were quietly or abruptly discontinued. From beloved snacks and beverages to toys and gadgets, browse what disappeared and what the brand said about it.
vanished-product.descLong: Sometimes the brand survives but the product doesn't. Vanished Products covers the individual SKUs, flavors, and models that were cut from lineups, often without much fanfare.

deregulation-era.label: Deregulation Era
deregulation-era.desc: Brands shaped or undone by industry deregulation—especially in aviation and finance. Browse the companies that thrived briefly after deregulation, then collapsed under new competitive pressures.
deregulation-era.descLong: Deregulation opened markets but also created volatility. In aviation especially, the post-1978 deregulation era saw rapid expansion followed by consolidation and bankruptcy.
